query: VHS scanlines TV static analog distortion CRT screen
message: sub:analog_degradation/VHS_CRT — anti_aesthetic — scanlines, TV static, color bars, pixel grids, retro CRT

response.objective_caption: An abstract composition featuring concentric elliptical bands in gradients of purple, magenta, and cyan-blue radiating symmetrically from a central vertical axis. A fine, regular grid of vertical lines overlays the entire frame, intersecting with the curved color bands. No recognizable objects, figures, or representational subjects are present; the image consists entirely of overlapping geometric patterns and color fields.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: An abstract, non-representational image composed of concentric elliptical bands in gradients of purple, magenta, and cyan-blue radiating symmetrically from a central vertical axis. Dense vertical scanlines and a visible pixel-grid texture overlay the entire frame, evoking analog CRT degradation and digital aliasing artifacts. The image abandons recognizable subject matter in favor of pure formal qualities—color, shape, and texture—while foregrounding the material imperfections of retro display technology and digital media.
